M0_briefing.notes: Briefing absorbed; design artifacts (algorithms.md, api.py.stub, package-layout.md, perf-budget.md, traces-to-plan.md) read into context.

M1_bootstrap.notes: pyproject deps + 8 package skeletons committed by previous impl-lead spawn. All M1 dep-vet conditions PASS (pip-audit, safety, license, pytest-asyncio 1.x asyncio_mode=auto, otel-instrumentation-logging 0.62b1 < 0.63).

M2_marker_scan.notes: Mode A (new package): light scan only. No prior markers exist in src/motadata_py_sdk/codec/. Ownership is pipeline-owned for the entire codec subtree.

M3_slice_1_codec.typecheck: mypy --strict: Success: no issues found in 6 source files
M3_slice_1_codec.alloc_summary_note: tracemalloc-measured allocs/op ≈ 0.01-0.09 across all benches because tracemalloc's compare_to(snap_before, snap_after) reports NET-RETAINED allocations after GC, not transient allocs created within an op. Strict allocs/op enforcement against design budget (30/4/35/4) defers to M3.5 sdk-profile-auditor which uses a different methodology.

M3_slice_2_events_utils.full_repo_tests: 558 passed (events.utils additions); 1 pre-existing flake in tests/bench/bench_acquire_contention.py::test_contention_throughput_meets_450k_per_sec_budget UNRELATED to nats-py-v1 (resourcepool host-load-flaky bench, M11 baseline 450k/CI floor 425k, observed 421k on this run host)
M3_slice_2_events_utils.typecheck: mypy --strict: Success: no issues found in 6 source files
M3_slice_2_events_utils.sentinel_count_components: 25 retryable + 9 non-retryable + 2 fail-fast wrappers (ErrCircuitOpen + ErrRateLimitExceeded relocated from middleware per CONV-12 fix D3 iter 3)
M3_slice_2_events_utils.alloc_summary_note: tracemalloc soft-gates pass (allocs/op ~0.0X for all three is_*  calls). Strict allocs_per_op==0 enforcement defers to M3.5 sdk-profile-auditor.

M2: inject_context does NOT parse traceparent (TracingMiddleware-equivalent gap)

M4: X-B3-ParentSpanId never written or read
M5: OTel-active path ignores manual TraceContext (algorithms.md §A2 lines 110-111)

context_var_identity_invariant_proven.test_str_slot_helpers_reexported_from_ev: PASS — events.core's set_tenant_id/get_tenant_id/set_correlation_id/get_correlation_id/set_message_id/get_message_id/set_reply_to/get_reply_to are the SAME function objects as events.utils's (asserted via `is`). Guarantees ONE ContextVar per slot SDK-wide; no silent propagation breaks.

asyncio_isolation_proven.test_trace_context_isolated_across_tasks: PASS — child task setting trace_context does not leak back to parent

M3_slice_4_events_corenats.full_repo_unit_plus_bench: 788 passed; 1 pre-existing flake (resourcepool bench_acquire_contention 367k vs 425k CI floor) UNRELATED to nats-py-v1 — same flake observed in slices 2 + 3
M3_slice_4_events_corenats.typecheck: mypy --strict: Success: no issues found in 7 source files (corenats)
perf_notes: All three hot-path microbenches measured against AsyncMock'd nats client (pure Python overhead). Real nats loopback numbers come from Phase 3 integration benches.
subscriber bare-path leaves 17.2us headroom under the 25us cap (CONSTRAINT-5 carry-over: ~13us reserved for slice-9 tracing wrap).
batch_publisher add p50=7.99us is at the 8us budget line on dev host; M3.5 sdk-profile-auditor will run on a dedicated host with strict comparison.
M3_slice_4_events_corenats.alloc_summary_note: tracemalloc soft-gates run; counts reflect NET-RETAINED measurement methodology (not transient allocs). Strict allocs/op enforcement vs design budget (12 publish / 2 add / 6 dispatch) defers to M3.5 sdk-profile-auditor (same methodology slices 1-3 use).

sentinel_args0_byte_exact_fix_applied.rationale: Prior spawn discovered _publisher.py raised SDK sentinels with adornment strings (e.g., raise ErrConnectionClosed('nats connection closed')), overriding the byte-exact .args[0] established by slice-2 _make_sentinel factory. This violates TPRD §4.6 cross-language wire-string equality.
sentinel_args0_byte_exact_fix_applied.fix_pattern: raise ErrXxx() (zero-arg, byte-exact .args[0] preserved) + log.warning(extra={...}) for diagnostic detail + 'from exc' chaining for __cause__.
violations_fixed_in_publisher_py: L165: ErrNotConnected('publisher has no NATS connection') -> ErrNotConnected()
L178: ErrConnectionClosed('nats connection closed') -> ErrConnectionClosed() from exc + WARN log
L180: ErrNotConnected('nats outbound buffer limit') -> ErrNotConnected() from exc + WARN log
L185: ErrPublishFailed(f'nats publish failed: {exc}') -> ErrPublishFailed() from exc + ERROR log
L236: ErrNotConnected('publisher has no NATS connection') -> ErrNotConnected()
L257: ErrRequestTimeout(f'nats request timed out after {timeout}s: {exc}') -> ErrRequestTimeout() from exc + WARN log
L261: ErrNoReply(f'nats no responders: {exc}') -> ErrNoReply() from exc + WARN log
L263: ErrConnectionClosed('nats connection closed') -> ErrConnectionClosed() from exc + WARN log
L268: ErrPublishFailed(f'nats request failed: {exc}') -> ErrPublishFailed() from exc + ERROR log
violations_fixed_in_subscriber_py: L175: ErrNotConnected('subscriber has no NATS connection') -> ErrNotConnected()
L180: ErrNotConnected('nats client not connected') -> ErrNotConnected()
sentinel_args0_byte_exact_fix_applied.violations_fixed_in_batch_publisher_py: none — batch_publisher already used Publisher's sentinels indirectly via .publish() and never raised SDK sentinels with adornment strings.

dd_1_invariant_proven.test_close_dd1_hook_fires_before_warn_lo: PASS — timeline-based test: spy on subscriber_mod._LOG.warning, assert hook entries appear in timeline before any 'errors draining subscriptions during close' warn entry
dd_1_invariant_proven.test_close_hook_exception_swallowed: PASS — bad hook raises ValueError; close completes; no recursion; logged via log.exception
dd_1_invariant_proven.test_close_no_hook_silent_default: PASS — None hook preserves Go-equivalent silent behavior

mirror_go_quirks_verified_by_tests: M1: Subscriber.unsubscribe(sub) named such, ACTUALLY calls sub.drain() (test_unsubscribe_actually_calls_drain)
M2: Subscriber.unsubscribe on non-_Subscription is silent no-op (test_unsubscribe_non_internal_silent_noop)
M3: Publisher handler errors not propagated to NATS - logged + swallowed (test_subscribe_handler_exception_swallowed_logged)
M4: Publisher.max_payload stored, not enforced in publish (test_max_payload_stored_but_not_enforced)
M5: BatchPublisher.add size-trigger flush uses fresh background ctx (test_size_trigger_flush_drops_caller_timeout)
M6: BatchPublisher has no max-buffer-size cap; 1000-element add succeeds (test_no_max_buffer_size_cap_sustained_add)
M7: BatchPublisher.add_multiple uses dict insertion order, NOT random (Go) order (test_add_multiple_preserves_dict_insertion_order)
tprd_15_FIX_scaffolds_left_for_slice_8_9: §15.28: messaging.system='nats' / messaging.operation='publish'/'receive'/'subscribe' attrs reserved in docstrings; spans NOT yet emitted (slice 9 OTel wiring)
M3_slice_4_events_corenats.h7b_mid_impl_checkpoint_status: Slice 4 is the H7b gate boundary per manifest. Recommend H7b before proceeding to slice 5.

symbol_status._note: Per-symbol status populated as each slice progresses. Symbols enumerated from design/api.py.stub (~224 public symbols across 8 new packages). Status values: pending, red, green, refactored, documented, marked, complete, blocked.
